sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 9:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 9:1
items.html: It indicates that it is a light without a vessel; hence, it cannot expand from above downward to the Guf. Its being under Malchut of the Rosh and above the vessels of the Guf indicates that it is a medium, an intermediate between the phase of the Rosh and the phase of the Guf, shining from its place to the Guf through its record that stands at the Keter of the Guf. (There)
